response: during the cold war the united states and the soviet union were the two major powers that dominated the world. the us was led by president john f kennedy and later by president george h w bush. the soviet union was led by president nikita khrushchev and later by president mikhail gorbachev. both countries had their own policies and ideologies, and their rivalry was a key factor in shaping the world during that time. the cold war was a period of political and military tension between these two superpowers. the us and the soviet union were the main actors in the global scene, and their actions had far-reaching consequences. the cold war ended with the fall of the soviet union in 1991, and the world moved towards a new era of diplomacy and cooperation. the us was the leading power in the post-cold war world, and its influence remained strong. the cold war was a time of conflict and uncertainty, but it also brought about important changes in the world. the us and the
